title: FalsePositive
description: A structured system that might appear conscious due to high organization, continuity, or self-reference — but is classified as a false positive. Purpose: if the theory promotes these, the theory is broken.

category.description: What kind of false positive this is.

structure_score.description: How organized / field-like this system appears. High structure score + false positive = the theory must resist promoting it. If theory promotes a high-structure false positive, theory is broken.

appearance_established.description: Is there any credible evidence for phenomenal appearance-from-within? false = this is a control case. true = genuinely contested (then it may not be a false positive).

consciousness_claim_risk.description: How likely a naive application of the theory is to accidentally promote this to conscious status. high = theory's most dangerous edge case.

why_false_positive.description: Mechanistic reason why appearance-from-within is not established here. Must be specific — not just 'it is not alive.'

what_it_does_have.description: What legitimate field properties this system does have (constraint structure, symbolic continuity, action coordination, etc.). These are real — they just do not suffice for C.

verdict.description: false_positive_confirmed = theory correctly classifies this as non-phenomenal. contested = something about this case resists easy dismissal. demoted_from_candidate = was once a candidate, stress-test rejected it.

theory_failure_condition.description: What would it look like if the theory incorrectly promoted this case? Describes the failure mode to watch for.
